title: Nosana
summary: Nosana is an open-source GPU cloud and marketplace for AI inference and other high-performance workloads. Arcium currently appears in official ecosystem coverage and recent Nosana community updates, but the public integration surface is still lightly specified.
description:
Nosana offers on-demand GPU compute and a marketplace for idle GPU supply, with the homepage emphasizing flexible pricing, global availability, and lower costs than traditional providers. Public materials position it as infrastructure for AI inference, training, rendering, and other high-performance compute tasks.

Arcium's Encrypted Ecosystem article lists Nosana among AI ecosystem partners, and Nosana's March 2026 monthly update explicitly mentions privacy-first AI with Arcium. That supports including Nosana in Atlas, but the public sources I reviewed still stop short of documenting a concrete end-user Arcium feature or workload flow.

bodySections.title: Network_Snapshot
bodySections.body: Nosana positions itself as an open-source GPU cloud for AI and high-performance workloads. The current homepage emphasizes run-on-demand GPU access, the ability to monetize idle hardware, up to 6x lower costs, and a live network snapshot spanning market cap, value secured, and active stakers.

bodySections.title: Arcium_Relevance
bodySections.body: Arcium lists Nosana among its AI ecosystem partners. Nosana's own March 2026 monthly update also references privacy-first AI with Arcium. That is enough to classify Nosana as an ecosystem project, but not enough to describe a fully documented integration surface yet.

statusNote: Arcium lists Nosana as an AI ecosystem partner, and Nosana's March 2026 monthly update references privacy-first AI with Arcium, but I did not find a public implementation page describing the exact integration surface.
